headline: Transcript: The geopolitics of chips — Taiwan’s ‘silicon shield’
summary: James Kynge talks to Kathrin Hille and Taiwan’s science and technology minister Cheng-Wen Wu
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Tech Tonic podcast episode: ‘The geopolitics of chips — Taiwan’s “silicon shield”’

[CLIP PLAYING]James KyngeIn Taiwan next summer, a new TV series will tackle a subject no one there likes to think about or talk about.

[‘ZERO DAY’ CLIP PLAYING]

Zero Day imagines the unthinkable — a Chinese invasion of Taiwan.

Audio clip from trailerTaiwan is not officially recognised as a sovereign nation by the United States. Behind me, we see many American citizens of Taiwanese heritage in line for evacuation. China’s recent blockade . . .

James KyngeThe trailer pulls no punches. The island’s stock market crashes, fighter jets scream overhead, communication lines are cut, and Beijing hacks Taiwan’s TV stations to show a smart and smiling Chinese news anchor telling Taiwanese people to accept a peaceful reunification with the motherland.

[‘ZERO DAY’ CLIP PLAYING]

Zero Day couldn’t be coming out at a more sensitive time. The world is changing. On the one hand, p
